title: Uniqueness in law criteria for weak solutions of multidimensional Ito SDEs
background_latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P)\) be a filtered probability space supporting a standard \(d\)-dimensional Brownian motion \(W\). Consider the It\^o stochastic differential equation (SDE)
\[
 dX_t = b(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad X_0=x\in\mathbb R^d,
\]
where \(b:[0,T]\times\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R^d\) and \(\sigma:[0,T]\times\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R^{d\times d}\) are Borel measurable coefficients. A \emph{weak solution} is a tuple \((\Omega',\mathcal F',(\mathcal F'_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P',W',X')\) such that \(W'\) is a Brownian motion and \(X'\) is an adapted continuous process satisfying the SDE in the It\^o sense under \(\mathbb P'\). 

The SDE is said to have \emph{uniqueness in law} (also called \emph{weak uniqueness}) if for any two weak solutions with the same initial condition \(x\), the laws of the solution processes \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) on path space coincide. One often assumes some form of (uniform) nondegeneracy/ellipticity of \(\sigma\), e.g. \(\sigma\sigma^\top\ge \lambda I\) for some \(\lambda>0\), but coefficients may still be merely measurable or discontinuous.

In the attached paper, the finite-player game dynamics lead to an \(n\)-dimensional SDE of the form
\[
 dX_t = D(t,X_t)\,dW_t,
\]
with diagonal diffusion matrix \(D(t,x)\) whose diagonal entries are bounded between two positive constants \(\sigma_1<\sigma_2\) (uniform ellipticity), but can be discontinuous as functions of \(x\) because they arise from feedback controls. The paper notes that, in dimensions greater than 2, general necessary-and-sufficient conditions for uniqueness in law of such weak solutions are not known.
problem_definition_latex:
For dimensions \(d>2\), characterize (with conditions that are simultaneously sufficient and necessary) when the It\^o SDE
\[
 dX_t = b(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad X_0=x\in\mathbb R^d,
\]
admits uniqueness in law of weak solutions.

In particular, determine such a sharp characterization for the driftless, uniformly elliptic case
\[
 dX_t = \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,
\]
including the case where \(\sigma(t,\cdot)\) may be discontinuous (e.g. piecewise constant) and may be restricted to be diagonal-valued.
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P)\) be a filtered probability space supporting a standard \(d\)-dimensional Brownian motion \(W\). Consider the It\^o stochastic differential equation (SDE)
\[
 dX_t = b(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad X_0=x\in\mathbb R^d,
\]
where \(b:[0,T]\times\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R^d\) and \(\sigma:[0,T]\times\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R^{d\times d}\) are Borel measurable coefficients. A \emph{weak solution} is a tuple \((\Omega',\mathcal F',(\mathcal F'_t)_{t\ge 0},\mathbb P',W',X')\) such that \(W'\) is a Brownian motion and \(X'\) is an adapted continuous process satisfying the SDE in the It\^o sense under \(\mathbb P'\). 

The SDE is said to have \emph{uniqueness in law} (also called \emph{weak uniqueness}) if for any two weak solutions with the same initial condition \(x\), the laws of the solution processes \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) on path space coincide. One often assumes some form of (uniform) nondegeneracy/ellipticity of \(\sigma\), e.g. \(\sigma\sigma^\top\ge \lambda I\) for some \(\lambda>0\), but coefficients may still be merely measurable or discontinuous.

In the attached paper, the finite-player game dynamics lead to an \(n\)-dimensional SDE of the form
\[
 dX_t = D(t,X_t)\,dW_t,
\]
with diagonal diffusion matrix \(D(t,x)\) whose diagonal entries are bounded between two positive constants \(\sigma_1<\sigma_2\) (uniform ellipticity), but can be discontinuous as functions of \(x\) because they arise from feedback controls. The paper notes that, in dimensions greater than 2, general necessary-and-sufficient conditions for uniqueness in law of such weak solutions are not known.

For dimensions \(d>2\), characterize (with conditions that are simultaneously sufficient and necessary) when the It\^o SDE
\[
 dX_t = b(t,X_t)\,dt + \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,\qquad X_0=x\in\mathbb R^d,
\]
admits uniqueness in law of weak solutions.

In particular, determine such a sharp characterization for the driftless, uniformly elliptic case
\[
 dX_t = \sigma(t,X_t)\,dW_t,
\]
including the case where \(\sigma(t,\cdot)\) may be discontinuous (e.g. piecewise constant) and may be restricted to be diagonal-valued.
open_problem_quote: It is still a problem to find conditions, sufficient and necessary at the same time, guaranteeing uniqueness in law of weak solutions of (2) in dimensions greater than 2 (see e.g. abstract of [23]).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for a sharp necessary-and-sufficient characterization across a broad class of multidimensional SDEs, not a single conjectured inequality or finite-dimensional extremal instance. Automated search might find examples of non-uniqueness/uniqueness for specific coefficient families, but there is no clear finite search space or decisive empirical test that would resolve the full characterization.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary loop could explore parametrized families of discontinuous or piecewise-constant diffusion coefficients \(\sigma\) and numerically probe for non-uniqueness indicators (e.g., by constructing distinct weak solutions via different approximation schemes) to suggest conjectures or counterexamples. However, uniqueness in law is not straightforward to certify computationally, and numerical evidence may be unreliable without rigorous analysis, limiting suitability to medium.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The open problem highlighted in the source paper concerns sharp (simultaneously necessary and sufficient) criteria for uniqueness in law of weak solutions to multidimensional It\^o SDEs with merely measurable, potentially discontinuous coefficients, even under uniform ellipticity. In the game-motivated setting, the diffusion matrix is diagonal with entries in \([\sigma_1,\sigma_2]\) but discontinuous in the state due to feedback (rank/threshold-type) controls; the paper stresses that while weak existence follows from classical uniformly elliptic theory (e.g. Krylov), weak uniqueness can fail in dimensions \(d>2\), and no general characterization is known.

Among the forward-citing items provided, the most relevant progress is confined to one-dimensional driftless threshold diffusions (oscillating Brownian motion type). "Mean-field ranking games with diffusion control" proves equilibrium existence/uniqueness and \(O(n^{-1/2})\) approximate Nash equilibria in a 1D setting with piecewise constant diffusion, relying on established 1D pathwise uniqueness results (Nakao-type). The other citing works are either methodological (PDE well-posedness for smooth controlled diffusions) or expository background, and do not address the multidimensional weak uniqueness characterization.

Overall, based on the provided forward citations, the problem remains open: there is still no known necessary-and-sufficient criterion for weak uniqueness for general uniformly elliptic, merely measurable (possibly diagonal/piecewise constant) diffusion coefficients in dimensions \(d>2\). Promising directions suggested by the source context include identifying structural subclasses (e.g. diagonal coefficients with special discontinuity geometry, or coefficients of bounded variation in each coordinate) where the martingale problem is well posed, and clarifying the boundary between well-posedness and counterexamples in higher dimensions.

sources.title: Mean-field ranking games with diffusion control
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proves existence and uniqueness of a mean field equilibrium for a 1-dimensional diffusion-control ranking game, where the equilibrium control is a threshold (bang-bang) diffusion coefficient. Concretely, for 
\[dX_t = m_b(X_t)\,dB_t,\qquad m_b(x)=\sigma_2\mathbf 1_{x\le b}+\sigma_1\mathbf 1_{x>b},\]
with an independent random horizon \(\tau\), there exists a unique \(b^*\in\mathbb R\) such that \(q(X^{b^*}_\tau,1-p)=b^*\), and \((\mathrm{Law}(X^{b^*}_\tau),m_{b^*})\) is a mean field equilibrium (Theorem 3.7). They also show the corresponding \(n\)-tuple of threshold controls is an \(\varepsilon_n\)-Nash equilibrium with \(\varepsilon_n\in O(n^{-1/2})\) (Theorem 4.1).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for sharp necessary-and-sufficient conditions for uniqueness in law of weak solutions to multidimensional (\(d>2\)) uniformly elliptic Itô SDEs with merely measurable/discontinuous diffusion (including diagonal/piecewise constant cases). This paper does not provide such a characterization. It only (i) notes that in \(n\ge 3\) uniqueness in law can fail even under uniform ellipticity (citing Nadirashvili), and (ii) works in a special 1-dimensional driftless setting where the diffusion is a discontinuous threshold function of bounded variation; in that case they rely on known 1D results (e.g. Nakao-type pathwise uniqueness) to assert existence of a unique strong solution for the oscillating Brownian motion SDE. Thus it provides a relevant special case and motivation, but no multidimensional uniqueness criteria.

sources.title: Diffusion control and games
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper proves well-posedness (existence and uniqueness) for a fully nonlinear second-order mean field games PDE system with controlled diffusion. Key PDE result: under structural assumptions (concavity, uniform ellipticity via

u>0\) and regularity of the Hamiltonians), the Hamilton–Jacobi equation \(u_t+H_1(t,x,
abla u)+H_2(t,x,\Delta u)+F(t,x,m(t))=0\), \(u(T,x)=G(x,m(T))\), admits a unique solution with high regularity \(u\in C^{1+\alpha/2,3+\alpha}\); then the coupled Fokker–Planck equation \(m_t-\Delta(m H_2{}_q(t,x,\Delta u))+\mathrm{div}(m H_1{}_p(t,x,
abla u))=0\) has a unique solution \(m\in C([0,T];\mathcal P(\mathbb R^d))\), yielding existence (and under monotonicity/strict concavity, uniqueness) of solutions to the MFG system (Theorem 6.4).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for sharp necessary-and-sufficient criteria for uniqueness in law of weak solutions to multidimensional It SDEs with merely measurable/discontinuous coefficients, especially driftless uniformly elliptic (possibly diagonal) diffusions. This paper does not address weak uniqueness for such SDEs. It assumes strong regularity/structure on the controlled-diffusion Hamiltonians to obtain classical/viscosity PDE solutions and then uses standard linear FokkerPlanck well-posedness under bounded, uniformly elliptic coefficients. The diffusion coefficients arising in their FP equation are functions like \(a(t,x)=H_2{}_q(t,x,\Delta u(t,x))\), which are bounded and (under their assumptions) regular, far from the discontinuous Borel setting. Thus it provides PDE techniques/tools relevant to controlled diffusion models, but no characterization of weak uniqueness for discontinuous uniformly elliptic SDEs in \(d>2\).

sources.title: Large ranking games
sources.theorem_or_result:
The talk identifies equilibrium/approximate equilibrium controls in a rank-based diffusion-control game where each player controls only the diffusion coefficient. In the two-player case with dynamics 
\[dX_t=a_1(X_t,Y_t)\,dW_t^1,\quad dY_t=a_2(X_t,Y_t)\,dW_t^2,\]
with measurable controls taking values in \([\sigma_1,\sigma_2]\), a saddle point is given by the discontinuous feedback 
\[a_1^*(x,y)=\sigma_2\mathbf 1_{\{x\le y\}}+\sigma_1\mathbf 1_{\{x>y\}},\quad a_2^*(x,y)=a_1^*(y,x).\]
In the mean-field limit, the best response to a threshold \(b\) for the objective \(\sup_\beta \mathbb P(X_T^\beta>b)\) is the threshold diffusion control \(m_b(x)=\sigma_2\mathbf 1_{\{x\le b\}}+\sigma_1\mathbf 1_{\{x>b\}}\), yielding an oscillating Brownian motion (OBM), and an equilibrium threshold \(b^*\) is given in closed form by solving \(\mathbb P(X_T^{m_{b^*}}>b^*)=\alpha\). The resulting profile in the \(n\)-player game is an \(\varepsilon_n\)-Nash equilibrium with \(\varepsilon_n=O(n^{-1/2})\).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for sharp necessary-and-sufficient conditions for uniqueness in law of weak solutions to multidimensional Itô SDEs with merely measurable/discontinuous (uniformly elliptic) diffusion, especially diagonal diffusion in dimensions \(d>2\). This citing item is a short talk/preprint summary about ranking games; it uses discontinuous, uniformly elliptic diffusion coefficients (piecewise constant threshold controls) and explicitly references oscillating Brownian motion in 1D and a 2D two-player system. However, it does not propose or prove any general uniqueness-in-law characterization in dimensions \(d>2\), nor does it establish new uniqueness results for the \(n\)-dimensional diagonal, discontinuous diffusion matrices arising in the game. At most, it provides motivating examples where weak solutions are considered under discontinuous diffusion controls and where well-posedness/uniqueness issues are relevant.
